headlines.headline: Aukus Nations Announce Underwater Drone Project to Protect Sea Cables
headlines.summary: The US, UK and Australia will develop unmanned undersea vehicles under the Aukus defense pact, with the UK contributing £150m. The technology aims to protect undersea cables and boost naval defense capabilities, with deployment expected by 2027.

perspectives.angle: AUKUS Partners Announce Underwater Drone Project to Protect Sea Cables
perspectives.summary: The US, UK and Australia unveiled plans to develop underwater drone technology under their AUKUS military pact, aimed at protecting undersea cables and boosting naval defense capabilities. UK will contribute £150m with the technology expected ready by next year.

headlines.headline: Israeli Soldiers Describe Ongoing Killings in Gaza Despite Ceasefire
headlines.summary: AP interviews with Israeli reservists reveal continued shooting of Palestinians near the 'yellow line' dividing Gaza. Over 900 people have been killed since October's ceasefire, with soldiers describing permissive rules of engagement and orders to 'shoot to kill' anyone crossing the line.

headlines.headline: Malaysia questions Norway's apology over cancelled missile deal
headlines.summary: Malaysian officials say Norway's apology for unilaterally cancelling the Naval Strike Missile export license has raised more questions, casting doubt on the future of the defense procurement.

headlines.headline: WHO Chief Visits Epicenter of Ebola Outbreak in DR Congo
headlines.summary: The WHO Director-General toured the heart of the Democratic Republic of Congos Ebola outbreak, warning that the spread is outpacing the global response and calling for more international funding.

perspectives.angle: Three Ebola vaccines in development amid DR Congo outbreak
perspectives.summary: IAVI, Moderna, and Oxford are racing to develop vaccines for the Bundibugyo Ebola strain, which has killed nearly 250 people. The outbreak threatens to rival the 2014-16 West Africa epidemic.

headlines.headline: Philippines Defense Chief Warns China Threat Persists Despite US-China Thaw
headlines.summary: Manilas defense minister said the Philippines remains under severe threat from China despite the recent Trump-Xi summit that eased tensions between Washington and Beijing.

headlines.headline: Austrian Protesters Block Key Alpine Motorway Connecting Germany and Italy
headlines.summary: Thousands of local residents shut down the Brenner motorway, a vital north-south corridor through the Alps, in protest against trucks and tourists clogging their roads.

headlines.headline: SoftBank to Invest €45 Billion in AI Data Centers in France
headlines.summary: Japan's SoftBank Group announced plans to invest €45 billion over five years to build artificial intelligence infrastructure in France, marking a major expansion of AI computing capacity in Europe.

headlines.headline: Iran Claims It Shot Down US Military Drone Over Territorial Waters
headlines.summary: Iranian military says it detected and destroyed an MQ-1 Predator UAV after it entered Iranian airspace on a hostile mission, escalating tensions as nuclear deal negotiations continue.

headlines.headline: Euphrates Flooding Displaces Thousands in Eastern Syria
headlines.summary: Syrian authorities opened dam spillways for the first time in three decades as rising Euphrates waters forced thousands to flee in Deir Ezzor province.

headlines.headline: US Military Fires on Blockade Runner Attempting to Reach Iranian Port
headlines.summary: U.S. Central Command disabled a Gambia-flagged vessel with a Hellfire missile after it ignored warnings while attempting to breach the American blockade of Iranian ports, marking an escalation in enforcement of the Strait of Hormuz closure.

perspectives.angle: US Military Stops Sixth Ship Attempting to Break Iran Blockade
perspectives.summary: A Gambian-flagged cargo vessel, the Lian Star, was disabled by a US missile strike after ignoring 20+ warnings while trying to reach an Iranian port. The US has now stopped six ships and redirected 116 since implementing the blockade on April 17.

perspectives.angle: Trump Holds Iran Deal Meeting as US Stops Another Blockade Runner
perspectives.summary: President Trump met with top aides in the White House Situation Room to decide on extending the Iran ceasefire, while US Central Command reported stopping a Gambian-flagged cargo ship attempting to breach the Iranian port blockade—the sixth ship interdicted since April.

headlines.headline: China Absent from Shangri-La Defense Forum as Delegates Ask Where is China
headlines.summary: Chinese Defense Minister Dong Jun skipped the annual Asian security summit again this year, sending only a low-profile delegation of PLA experts and scholars as Beijing maintains its absence from key regional defense dialogues.

headlines.headline: SoftBank Plans $88 Billion AI Data Hub in France
headlines.summary: SoftBank founder Masayoshi Son announced the massive investment after meeting President Macron, with France's status as an energy exporter playing a key role in the decision.

headlines.headline: China Building Launch Pads Near Nuclear Missile Silos
headlines.summary: Satellite images reveal Beijing is building a web of military sites near its nuclear silos, with launch pads that could enhance China's strategic capabilities as its missiles can reach any US city.

headlines.headline: US General Holds Rare Meeting With Cuban Military Officials Near Guantanamo
headlines.summary: US Southern Command commander met with Cuban military officials in the first such high-level meeting in recent memory, amid Cuban concerns about possible US military action.

headlines.headline: Trump roils waters on Taiwan independence after Beijing summit
headlines.summary: Debate over US president's China trip reveals varied interpretations of his stance on Taiwan, with Trump saying the US is "not looking to have somebody say, let's go independent."

headlines.headline: US general holds rare meeting with Cuban military officials near Guantanamo
headlines.summary: The first meeting in recent memory between a US Southern Command head and Cuban military officials comes amid growing concerns in Cuba about possible US military action.
